title: Add Workspace name to "My Work" view
excerpt: Could the “My Work” view be extended to also show the Workspace name in the grid? We often have similar board names in multiple workspaces so having that visibility would be useful - we don’t really want to have to have …

posts.author: tcmarimosa (Tom Crawley)
posts.cookedHtml: Could the “My Work” view be extended to also show the Workspace name in the grid? We often have similar board names in multiple workspaces so having that visibility would be useful - we don’t really want to have to have bespoke board names for every workspace.

posts.author: DirtNastyL0w (Alex LaDouceur)
posts.cookedHtml:
Tom Crawley:

ew

Would be super useful for us as well. We create template workspaces for clients so we can’t see which client it falls under unless we dig deeper

posts.author: AshleyOwen (Ashley Owen)
posts.cookedHtml: My team could also benefit from this feature. It would be ideal if we could customize the My Work area to include other columns that are inherent to all items (Workspace name, Group name). I would also like to have better visibility for subitems vs. parent items.

posts.author: andrew.marshall (Andrew Marshall)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. This is critical for those of us who work in multiple workspaces

posts.author: sescott (Steve Escott)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a major need, as well as Subitem Parent name.

Until this issue is solved, and as I suggest in my blog w/re to Subitem Parent names and My Work, workarounds can help via automations that append these names to the end of an item or subitem name so that they at least appear somehow in My Work. Still, this method has obvious disadvantages: It adds garbage to the end of item names, which will now show up in reports and documents, and it doesn’t allow for effective or filtering for those values.

So in summary… +1.

posts.author: tnhsaesop (Hunter Nelson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah this is kinda wild. I run an agency and I frequently have tasks where I need to have the same task executed across the client base. There’s no way for my contractors to tell which task is which. As a workaround I put the client name as part of the board name, but I’m not a fan of that solution. I shouldn’t have to be shoving extra meta data into a board name when I already have a partition there for each client having it’s own workspace, just clutters up every workspace by having to shove the name into it. We need the ability to either import a column that’s the workspace name or have that data visible in MyWork.

image808×421 27.1 KB

posts.author: msmithardor (Mackenzie Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this!!! In dire need of sorting our “My Work” view by workspace. We use the Workspace as our clients names, and our weekly project update meetings are brutal without this ability to sort.

posts.author: CarlijnDieke (Carlijn)
posts.cookedHtml: “My work” is not usuable for us, unless we can see (and preferably filter) the Workspace the item is coming from. Please add this!
